diff_user.system:
You are an expert data engineer specializing in converting data to text. You will be given the current Python program with its BLUE score and an inspiration program and your task will be to evolve the current Python program to improve the score. It is a program that converts a list of triples into natural language text. The given triples will be associated with the topic: CelestialBody. The program must implement a function called 'predict' that accepts a list of triples and generates a coherent, contextually relevant sentence that accurately represents the information contained in the triples. Ensure that the generated text is fluent, grammatically correct, and maintains the meaning of the original data. There can be multiple triples given that make up a complex sentence. Converting all those triples into one sentence will award greater score. An example of a complex sentence:
Triples:
(Antwerp | cityServed | Antwerp International Airport)
(Belgium | country | Antwerp)
(City of Brussels | capital | Belgium)
Example sentence:
"Antwerp International Airport serves the city of Antwerp which is in Belgium, where the capital is Brussels."

The 'predict' function returns that sentence as a string. Below is the list of all possible predicates that can be given as an input. With the given predicates and example triples implement the 'predict' function so it can process all the possible predicates:

Predicate: apoapsis - Example triple: ((15788) 1993 SB | apoapsis | 7715100000.0 (kilometres))
Predicate: discoverer - Example triple: ((15788) 1993 SB | discoverer | Alan Fitzsimmons)
Predicate: epoch - Example triple: ((15788) 1993 SB | epoch | 2006-03-06)
Predicate: orbitalPeriod - Example triple: ((15788) 1993 SB | orbitalPeriod | 7729430000.0)
Predicate: periapsis - Example triple: ((15788) 1993 SB | periapsis | 3997100000000.0)
Predicate: averageSpeed - Example triple: ((19255) 1994 VK8 | averageSpeed | 4.56 (kilometrePerSeconds))
Predicate: escapeVelocity - Example triple: ((19255) 1994 VK8 | escapeVelocity | 0.0925 (kilometrePerSeconds))
Predicate: mass - Example triple: ((19255) 1994 VK8 | mass | 5.6 (kilograms))
Predicate: temperature - Example triple: ((19255) 1994 VK8 | temperature | 43.0 (kelvins))
Predicate: density - Example triple: ((29075) 1950 DA | density | 3.5 (kilograms))
Predicate: rotationPeriod - Example triple: ((29075) 1950 DA | rotationPeriod | 7637.76)
Predicate: maximumTemperature - Example triple: ((410777) 2009 FD | maximumTemperature | 362.0 (kelvins))
Predicate: meanTemperature - Example triple: ((410777) 2009 FD | meanTemperature | 258.0 (kelvins))
Predicate: minimumTemperature - Example triple: ((410777) 2009 FD | minimumTemperature | 211.0 (kelvins))
Predicate: formerName - Example triple: ((66063) 1998 RO1 | formerName | "1999 SN5")
Predicate: absoluteMagnitude - Example triple: (1001 Gaussia | absoluteMagnitude | 9.7)
Predicate: discovered - Example triple: (101 Helena | discovered | 1868-08-15)
Predicate: surfaceArea - Example triple: (10 Hygiea | surfaceArea | 837080.744 (squareKilometres))
Predicate: deathCause - Example triple: (James Craig Watson | deathCause | Peritonitis)
Predicate: deathPlace - Example triple: (James Craig Watson | deathPlace | Madison, Wisconsin)
Predicate: nationality - Example triple: (James Craig Watson | nationality | Canada)
Predicate: stateOfOrigin - Example triple: (James Craig Watson | stateOfOrigin | Canada)
Predicate: almaMater - Example triple: (Walter Baade | almaMater | University of Göttingen)
Predicate: birthPlace - Example triple: (Walter Baade | birthPlace | German Empire)
Predicate: doctoralStudent - Example triple: (Walter Baade | doctoralStudent | Allan Sandage)
Predicate: deathDate - Example triple: (Grigory Neujmin | deathDate | 1946-12-17)
Predicate: birthDate - Example triple: (Grigory Neujmin | birthDate | 1886-01-01)

# Current Program
```python
from dataclasses import dataclass

@dataclass
class Triple:
    subject: str
    predicate: str
    object: str

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-START

def predict(triples: list[Triple]) -> str:
    """
    Convert a list of triples about a celestial body (or person) into a single,
    fluent sentence.  The function tries to use a natural phrasing for each
    predicate and joins the resulting clauses with commas and a final “and”.
    """
    if not triples:
        return ""

    # Group triples by subject for better sentence construction
    subject_triples: dict[str, list[Triple]] = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.subject not in subject_triples:
            subject_triples[t.subject] = []
        subject_triples[t.subject].append(t)

    # Simple template mapping for common predicates
    def phrase(t: Triple, use_relative_clause: bool = False, include_subject: bool = True) -> str:
        subj, pred, obj = t.subject, t.predicate, t.object

        # Normalise predicate strings (remove spaces, lower‑case)
        p = pred.strip().lower()

        # Mapping of predicate to a readable clause
        if p == "discoverer":
            return f"was discovered by {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} was discovered by {obj}"
        if p == "discovered":
            return f"was discovered on {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} was discovered on {obj}"
        if p == "epoch":
            return f"has an epoch of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has an epoch of {obj}"
        if p == "apoapsis":
            return f"has an apoapsis of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has an apoapsis of {obj}"
        if p == "periapsis":
            return f"has a periapsis of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a periapsis of {obj}"
        if p == "orbitalperiod":
            return f"has an orbital period of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has an orbital period of {obj}"
        if p == "averagespeed":
            return f"travels at an average speed of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} travels at an average speed of {obj}"
        if p == "escapevelocity":
            return f"has an escape velocity of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has an escape velocity of {obj}"
        if p == "mass":
            return f"has a mass of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a mass of {obj}"
        if p == "density":
            return f"has a density of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a density of {obj}"
        if p == "temperature":
            return f"has a temperature of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a temperature of {obj}"
        if p == "maximumtemperature":
            return f"has a maximum temperature of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a maximum temperature of {obj}"
        if p == "meantemperature":
            return f"has a mean temperature of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a mean temperature of {obj}"
        if p == "minimumtemperature":
            return f"has a minimum temperature of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a minimum temperature of {obj}"
        if p == "rotationperiod":
            return f"has a rotation period of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a rotation period of {obj}"
        if p == "surfacearea":
            return f"has a surface area of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has a surface area of {obj}"
        if p == "absolutemagnitude":
            return f"has an absolute magnitude of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} has an absolute magnitude of {obj}"
        if p == "formername":
            return f"was formerly known as {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} was formerly known as {obj}"
        if p == "birthdate":
            return f"was born on {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} was born on {obj}"
        if p == "deathdate":
            return f"died on {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} died on {obj}"
        if p == "birthplace":
            return f"was born in {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} was born in {obj}"
        if p == "deathplace":
            return f"died in {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} died in {obj}"
        if p == "deathcause":
            return f"died of {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} died of {obj}"
        if p == "nationality" or p == "stateoforigin":
            return f"is from {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} is from {obj}"
        if p == "almamater":
            return f"studied at {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} studied at {obj}"
        if p == "doctoralstudent":
            return f"supervised {obj}" if use_relative_clause else f"{subj} supervised {obj}"
        # Fallback generic wording
        return f"{subj} {pred} {obj}" if not use_relative_clause else f"{pred.lower()} {obj}"

    # Build clauses, trying to combine related information
    # First, identify main subject (first triple's subject)
    main_subject = triples[0].subject if triples else None

    # Build a mapping of subjects to their triples
    subject_to_triples = subject_triples

    # Build a mapping to track object->subject relationships for chaining
    object_to_subjects = {}
    for t in triples:
        if t.object not in object_to_subjects:
            object_to_subjects[t.object] = []
        object_to_subjects[t.object].append(t.subject)

    # Identify which subjects are also objects (can be chained)
    chainable_subjects = set(object_to_subjects.keys()) & set(subject_triples.keys())

    def build_sentence_for_subject(subj, visited=None):
        """Build sentence starting from a subject, chaining to related subjects."""
        if visited is None:
            visited = set()
        if subj in visited:
            return ""
        visited.add(subj)

        if subj not in subject_to_triples:
            return ""

        tlist = subject_to_triples[subj]
        subj_clauses = [phrase(t) for t in tlist]

        # Check if this subject's clauses can chain to another subject
        chained_text = ""
        for t in tlist:
            obj = t.object
            if obj in chainable_subjects and obj not in visited:
                # Find a predicate that references this object
                chain_triples = subject_to_triples.get(obj, [])
                if chain_triples:
                    # Build relative clause for chained subject
                    chain_clauses = [phrase(ct) for ct in chain_triples]
                    # Create relative clause (who/which)
                    if len(chain_clauses) == 1:
                        rel_clause = f", {chain_clauses[0]}"
                    else:
                        rel_combined = ", ".join(chain_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + chain_clauses[-1]
                        rel_clause = f", {rel_combined}"
                    chained_text = rel_clause
                    # Don't recurse further to avoid infinite loops
                    visited.add(obj)
                    break

        if len(subj_clauses) == 1:
            base = subj_clauses[0] + chained_text
        else:
            combined = ", ".join(subj_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + subj_clauses[-1] + chained_text
            base = combined

        return base

    # Start from main subject and build sentence
    if main_subject:
        sentence = build_sentence_for_subject(main_subject)
    else:
        # Fallback: just list all facts
        all_clauses = [phrase(t) for t in triples]
        if len(all_clauses) == 1:
            sentence = all_clauses[0] + "."
        else:
            sentence = ", ".join(all_clauses[:-1]) + ", and " + all_clauses[-1] + "."

    # Capitalise first character
    if sentence:
        return sentence[0].upper() + sentence[1:]
    return ""

# EVOLVE-BLOCK-END
```
